title: China: Long-game growth strategy – Standard Chartered
snippet: Standard Chartered economists Carol Liao and Shuang Ding expect China to set a 2026 GDP growth target of 4.5–5.0% at the National People’s Congress, slightly below 2025.

title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Rebounds on firm USD, targets 0.7800
snippet: USD/CHF recovers some ground on Thursday, rising some 0.19% as the Greenback appreciates and bounces off daily lows beneath 0.7700 on solid US data. At the time of writing, the pair trades at 0.7743, yet it remains shy of the key resistance level seen at 0.7817, the February 2 daily peak.

title: Fed’s Goolsbee: I want us to be careful
snippet: Austan Goolsbee, President of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago, said interest rates can come down, but they don't want to front-load before inflation eases. He also added he wants the Fed to be careful in a Fox News Interview on Thursday.

title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD slides amid a firm US Dollar
snippet: Silver (XAG/USD) price loses its brightness on Thursday, moving in the opposite direction of Gold, which remains steady as the US Dollar recovers some ground and rises. Stronger-than-expected US jobs data keeps the white metal pressured in the mid-North American session.

title: Gold holds above $5,170 as Iran tensions offset a firm USD
snippet: Gold price stays firm on Thursday during the North American session as geopolitical tensions remain elevated despite the beginning of the third round of talks between the US and Iran in Geneva. Also, solid data from the US kept bullion prices contained.

title: AUD/USD declines on USD strength, hawkish RBA outlook sustains
snippet: AUD/USD trades with a negative bias on Thursday as broad-based US Dollar (USD) strength weighs on the Australian Dollar (AUD). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 0.7078, down 0.60%.

title: USD/JPY retreats after two-day rise amid US labor resilience, BoJ rate hike hints
snippet: USD/JPY trades around 156.20 on Thursday at the time of writing, down 0.15% on the day, slightly correcting after two consecutive days of gains.

title: USD/JPY: Watching 152–159.50 range break – Scotiabank
snippet: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret note modest Japanese Yen gains versus the Dollar, noting outperformance within the G10 but only a partial recovery of recent BoJ‑related losses.

title: GBP/USD slips as firm US jobless claims offset BoE cut bets
snippet: The Pound Sterling (GBP) retreats over 0.11% on Thursday as the Greenback remains steady following a report that the number of Americans applying for unemployment benefits was below estimates last week, an indication of the resilience of the labor market.

title: Gold steadies as USD gains, geopolitical risks cap downside
snippet: Gold (XAU/USD) gives up earlier intraday gains on Thursday, coming under modest pressure as the US Dollar gains momentum. The metal remains confined within this week’s trading range, with markets cautious ahead of key geopolitical developments.

title: USD/CAD rises toward 1.3700 as US Dollar trims earlier weakness
snippet: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) pares earlier gains against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday as the Greenback shrugs off its intraday weakness. At the time of writing, USD/CAD trades at 1.3704, rebounding from the daily low around 1.3659.

title: US: Midterms, Fed leadership and USD risk – TD Securities
snippet: TD Securities argues US political dynamics will shape Dollar and EMFX performance into 2026.

title: Fed’s Miran: The Fed should cut a percentage point this year
snippet: Federal Reserve (Fed) Governor Stephen Miran said that he has not seen anything worrisome yet in private credit despite some bumps, and added that prices right now seem stable, in an interview with Fox Business on Thursday.

title: AUD/USD: Positive AUD theme despite fading risk – BNY
snippet: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage flags AUD/USD as another Dollar-positive rebalancing candidate. While AUD has seen mostly net buying since late January, inflows have been light and positioning less stretched than in other carry or hard-asset currencies.

title: USD/CHF rebounds after consecutive losses amid US labor data, safe-haven flows
snippet: USD/CHF trades around 0.7740 on Thursday at the time of writing, up 0.15% on the day, snapping a series of consecutive losses. The pair is supported by a modest rebound in the US Dollar (USD), although the broader backdrop remains characterized by strong demand for safe-haven assets.

title: Fed: Steady policy with later cuts – TD Securities
snippet: TD Securities’ Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir argue that a stable US labor market and contained inflation should keep the Federal Reserve on hold in the near term, even as growth remains firm.

title: No Fed, no safety net: Why private credit’s first real recession will be its moment of truth
snippet: Private credit is easy to enter but hard to exit. Retail investors seeking fast cash could trigger a financial crisis.

title: These increasingly popular loans can save you thousands of dollars in interest — or double your debt. Here’s when they work.
snippet: A personal loan can be a powerful tool for consolidating high-interest debt, but it’s far from a cure-all.

snippet: Rupee settles at 90.96 against the dollar, influenced by crude oil prices and foreign fund flows.

title: Rupee rises 6 paise to 90.89 against US dollar in early trade
snippet: The rupee gained 6 paise to 90.89 against the US dollar in early trade on Wednesday aided by a weaker greenback and a strong opening in the domestic equity markets.

title: Czech Republic: Fiscal stance stays only slightly looser – ING
snippet: ING analysts Frantisek Taborsky and David Havrlant say Czech fiscal policy has loosened only marginally after the election, with the public finance deficit seen at 2.2% of GDP in 2026 and risks tilted higher.

title: LNG: Oil-indexed contracts transmit Iran shock – Rabobank
snippet: Rabobank’s energy strategists Joe DeLaura and Florence Schmit argue that global LNG markets, especially in Europe and Asia, would be pulled higher by Oil if Strait of Hormuz flows are threatened.

title: EUR/USD declines as US Dollar strengthens on Fed rate-cut outlook, weak Euro data
snippet: EUR/USD eases on Thursday, trimming earlier intraday gains as a firmer US Dollar (USD) weighs on the Euro (EUR). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 1.1794, retreating from the daily high of 1.1829.

title: Eurozone: Financial impulse points to solid growth – Commerzbank
snippet: Commerzbank’s Dr. Marco Wagner introduces a new financial impulse indicator based on the Fed's FCI-G indicator that signals how strongly the financial environment is driving or slowing down the economy.
